* Distances to property lines may be amended by the authority having jurisdiction.
† If tanks of a multiple tank installation are installed on a common concrete/steel base or pier, the clearances may be reduced to a 1/4 of the sum of diameters of the adjacent container.
‡ 10 ft (3 m) from any source of ignition.
§ At the discretion of the authority having jurisdiction.

title: Clearances for consumer tanks more than 125 USWG (475 L)
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Notwithstanding the requirements in Clause 7.10.2, a) a maximum of four tanks, each less than 125 USWG (475 L), that are manifolded together to form a system may be located against a common wall of a building. Not more than one such manifold system may be located against a common wall of a building unless separated by a clearance of 10 ft (3 m); and b) when the single largest tank in a manifolded system is allowed in item a), the aggregate capacity is not used when determining the minimum distances between the tanks and a property line, a building, a building opening or a source of ignition. The largest single tank in the manifolded system shall be used to determine the distances required in Table 7.4.

title: Clearances for consumer tanks less than 125 USWG (475 L)
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Notwithstanding the requirements in Clause 7.10.2, a) for capacities over 125 USWG (475 L) and up to and including 500 USWG (1900 L), the distance from a building wall may be reduced to 3 ft (1 m) for a single tank, provided that i) the building wall is of concrete or masonry construction; ii) there is a minimum of 10 ft (3 m) to the nearest building opening; and iii) the tank is used only for vapour service; and b) for capacities over 125 USWG (475 L) and up to and including 5000 USWG (19 000 L), the distances from a building wall of other than concrete or masonry construction may be reduced to the distances specified for concrete or masonry wall construction if protection acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction is provided.

title: Tank supports
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Tank flotation prevention
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Secure anchorage or sufficient pier height shall be provided for each tank to prevent flotation when high flood water can occur.

title: Horizontal tank with a capacity of 2000 USWG (7500 L) or less
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A horizontal tank with a capacity of 2000 USWG (7500 L) or less shall a) be mounted on a maximum of two supports, and these supports shall be of noncombustible material in the form of piers, pads, saddles, blocks, steel beam skid rails, or concrete, each of sufficient strength to support the weight of the tank when filled to capacity with propane; b) not be installed with an individual block or pad at each leg of the tank; c) have the top of any support not less than 3 in (75 mm) above grade, and the clearances between the bottom of the tank and grade shall not be less than 6 in (150 mm); and d) be installed with a maximum distance of 30 in (750 mm) between the bottom of the tank and a concrete pad, slab, or grade.

title: Vertical tank
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A vertical tank of 125 USWG (475 L) or less shall be installed in accordance with Clause 6.7.1. A vertical tank greater than 125 USWG (475 L) capacity shall be secured to a reinforced concrete base (see Figure B.2) that a) extends at least 1 ft (300 mm) from all sides of the tank surface of the pad and the above grade level; b) has footings located below the frost line that are designed to have a maximum bearing pressure on the soil of 2000 lb/ft2 (95 kPa); c) limits the distance between the bottom of the tank and the concrete pad to a maximum of 30 in (750 mm); and d) is subject to the approval of the authority having jurisdiction.

title: Horizontal tank with a capacity greater than 2000 USWG (7500 L)
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A horizontal tank with a capacity greater than 2000 USWG (7500 L) shall a) be mounted on no more than two concrete saddles or piers; b) have footings located below the frost line that are designed for a maximum bearing pressure on the soil of not more than 2000 lb/ft2 (95 kPa); c) have corrosion protection provided on that portion of the tank in contact with concrete piers or saddles; and d) have a distance of not less than 24 in (600 mm) between the underside of the tank and the grade level.

title: Skid frame
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Notwithstanding the requirements in Clause 7.11.4, a horizontal tank with a capacity greater than 2000 USWG (7500 L) may be mounted on a skid frame, provided that a) the skid frame assembly is designed to accommodate anticipated loads; b) the skid frame assembly is installed on a concrete slab, foundation, or compacted base designed to accommodate static and dynamic loads imposed by the tank and skid frame; and c) steps are taken to allow for differential movement between any fixed piping and the skid frame assembly.

title: Filling plants and refill centres
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Lighting
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Lighting shall be provided to illuminate the tank area, including control valves and other equipment, when loading or unloading is done during other than daylight hours.

title: Means of access
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Means of access for firefighting, using a fire extinguisher or fire department apparatus, shall be provided to the storage tank areas or buildings.

title: No smoking signs
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Prominently displayed "NO SMOKING" signs or symbols shall be installed at all entrances and points of transfer. Lettering shall be a minimum of 4 in (100 mm) high and shall be red on a white background. Symbols shall be a minimum of 7-1/2 in (190 mm) in diameter. Note: The equivalent French wording is "DEFENSE DE FUMER".

title: Clearance from any drain, sewer, or pit
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A tank and its equipment shall be located not less than 25 ft (7.6 m) from any drain, sewer, or pit unless the drain, sewer, or pit is equipped with an automatic flammable vapour leak detection system.

title: Transfer connection location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Except at container refill centres, a tank truck or cargo liner transfer connection shall be located not less than a) 25 ft (7.6 m) from the line of adjoining property and any source of ignition; b) 10 ft (3 m) from any pump or compressor if housed in one or more separate buildings; c) 10 ft (3 m) from any storage tank; and d) 10 ft (3 m) from a common 2-hour fire-rated building.

title: Tank above another tank
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A tank shall not be installed above another tank.

title: Heavily populated or congested areas
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: In heavily populated or congested areas, the authority having jurisdiction may determine restrictions of individual tank capacity, total storage, distance to line of adjoining property, and other requirements.

title: Heating
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A container filling or storage building shall not contain a heating plant or any source of ignition and shall not be heated except by indirect means.

title: Transfer hoses
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A transfer hose used for loading or unloading a storage tank shall be disconnected from a cargo liner, tank truck, or tank car immediately after transfer operations have been completed. A cargo liner, tank truck, or tank car shall have all transfer hoses disconnected from an attendant is not present.

title: Transfer valves
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Transfer valves and the electrical system controlling the pumping equipment shall be turned off when not in use.

title: Signage
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Signage shall be prominently displayed on or adjacent to a tank and visible from the filling location, and shall display: "NO SMOKING -- TURN OFF ALL SOURCES OF IGNITION"* in red lettering that is a minimum of 2 in (50 mm) high on a white background, or recognized symbols for no smoking and no ignition that are a minimum of 4 in (100 mm) in diameter. For facilities where cylinders are filled, a sign shall be prominently displayed and be worded as follows: "TRANSPORT CYLINDERS SECURED IN AN UPRIGHT POSITION IN A VENTILATED SPACE"* in red lettering that is a minimum of 1 in (25 mm) high on a white background. Lettering shall be red on a white background. Note: The equivalent French wording is "DEFENSE DE FUMER -- COUPER TOUTES LES SOURCES D'ALLUMAGE" / "TRANSPORTER LES BOUTEILLES EN POSITION VERTICALE DANS UN ENDROIT AERE".

title: Filling capacity sign
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A sign shall be prominently displayed on or adjacent to a tank and visible from the filling location, and shall be worded as follows: "IT IS AN OFFENCE TO FILL PROPANE CYLINDERS AND MOTOR FUEL CONTAINERS IN EXCESS OF 80% CAPACITY BY VOLUME."* Lettering shall be a minimum of 1 in (25 mm) high and shall be red on a white background. Note: The equivalent French wording is "IL EST INTERDIT DE REMPLIR LES BOUTEILLES DE PROPANE ET LES RESERVOIRS DE CARBURANT POUR MOTEURS A UNE CAPACITE DEPASSANT 80% DE LEUR CAPACITE TOTALE EN VOLUME."

title: Differential movement
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: There shall be provision in the design of the installation of the tank and pumping equipment to permit differential movement.

title: Liquid withdrawal opening
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: The liquid withdrawal opening shall be equipped with an internal safety control valve or an internal excess-flow valve with a remotely operated valve at the emergency shut-off type installed within 6 in (150 mm) of the excess-flow valve.

title: Electrical equipment at filling plants
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Electrical equipment classification
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description:
Electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures at filling plants (bulk plants) shall be classified in accordance with Table 7.6.

[Table 7.6]
Table 7.6 - Electrical classification
(See Clauses 6.5.1.11, 6.5.1.12, 6.5.2.6.1, 7.13, 7.19.5, and 8.8.1.1.)
The values in this table are metric and are the standard. US customary is given for reference only.
| Part | Location | Extent of hazardous location* | Group IIA hazardous location† |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | Storage containers other than cylinders and ASME vertical containers of less than 454 kg (1000 lb) water capacity | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from connections, except connections otherwise covered in this table | Zone 2 |
| B | Tank vehicle and tank car loading and unloading‡ | Within 3 m (10 ft) in all directions from connections regularly made or disconnected for product transfer | Zone 1 |
| B |  | Beyond 3 m (10 ft) but within 7.5 m (25 ft) in all directions from a point where connections are regularly made or disconnected and within the cylindrical volume between the horizontal equator of the sphere and grade (See Figure F.1) | Zone 2 |
| C | Gauge vent openings other than those on cylinders and ASME vertical containers of less than 454 kg (1000 lb) water capacity | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from point of discharge | Zone 1 |
| C |  | Beyond 1.5 m (5 ft) but within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from point of discharge | Zone 2 |
| D | Relief device discharge other than those on cylinders and ASME vertical containers of less than 454 kg (1000 lb) water capacity | Within the direct path of discharge | Zone 1 -- Note: Fixed electrical equipment should not be installed. |
| D |  | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from point of discharge | Zone 1 |
| D |  | Beyond 1.5 m (5 ft) but within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from point of discharge except within the direct path of discharge | Zone 2 |
| E | Pumps, vapour compressors, gas/air mixers, and vaporizers (other than direct-fired or indirect-fired with an attached or adjacent gas-fired heat source) in one of the following conditions: a) indoors without ventilation; | Entire room and any adjacent room not separated by a gas-tight partition | Zone 1 |
| E | a) indoors without ventilation; | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) of the exterior side of any exterior wall or roof that is not vapour-tight or within 4.5 m (15 ft) of any exterior opening (See Figure F.2) | Zone 2 |
| E | b) indoors with adequate ventilation; or | Entire room and any adjacent room not separated by a gas-tight partition | Zone 2 |
| E | c) outdoors in open air at or above grade. | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from this equipment and within the cylindrical volume between the horizontal equator of the sphere and grade | Zone 2 |
| F | Service station vehicle fuel dispenser units | Entire space within vehicle fuel dispenser enclosure, or up to a solid partition within the enclosure at any height above the base; the space within 450 mm (18 in) horizontally from the vehicle fuel dispenser enclosure up to 1.2 m (4 ft) above the base or to the height of a solid partition within the enclosure; entire pit or open space beneath the vehicle fuel dispenser | Zone 1 |
| F |  | The space above a solid partition within the vehicle fuel dispenser enclosure; the space up to 450 mm (18 in) above grade within 6 m (20 ft) horizontally from any edge of the vehicle fuel dispenser enclosure | Zone 2 |
| G | Pits or trenches containing, or located beneath propane gas valves, pumps, vapour compressors, regulators, and similar equipment -- a) without mechanical ventilation; or | Entire pit or trench | Zone 1 |
| G | a) without mechanical ventilation; or | Entire room and any adjacent room not separated by a gas-tight partition | Zone 2 |
| G | a) without mechanical ventilation; or | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from pit or trench when located outdoors | Zone 2 |
| G | b) with adequate mechanical ventilation. | Entire pit or trench | Zone 2 |
| G | b) with adequate mechanical ventilation. | Entire room and any adjacent room not separated by a gas-tight partition | Zone 2 |
| G | b) with adequate mechanical ventilation. | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from pit or trench when located outdoors | Zone 2 |
| H | Special buildings or rooms for storage of portable containers | Entire room | Zone 2 |
| I | Pipelines and connections containing operational bleeds, drips, vents, or drains | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from point of discharge | Zone 1 |
| I |  | Beyond 1.5 m (5 ft) from point of discharge (extent of classified areas characterized as they are in Part E) | As shown in Part E, as applicable |
| J | Container filling: a) indoors with adequate ventilation§; or | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from connections regularly made or disconnected for product transfer | Zone 1 |
| J | a) indoors with adequate ventilation§; or | Beyond 1.5 m (5 ft) and entire room | Zone 2 |
| J | b) outdoors in open air. | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from the dispensing hose inlet connections for product transfer | Zone 1 |
| J | b) outdoors in open air. | Beyond 1.5 m (5 ft) but within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from the dispensing hose inlet connections and within the cylindrical volume between the horizontal equator of the sphere and grade (See Figure F.3) | Zone 2 |
| K | Outdoor cylinder storage: aggregate storage of propane -- a) up to and including 454 kg (1000 lb) water capacity; or | Within 1.5 m (5 ft) in all directions from connections | Zone 2 |
| K | b) greater than 454 kg (1000 lb) water capacity. | Within 4.5 m (15 ft) in all directions from connections | Zone 2 |
* The classified area shall not extend beyond an unpierced wall, roof, or solid vapour-tight partition.
† See the Canadian Electrical Code, Part I, for definitions of classes, groups, and zones. Most of the flammable vapours and gases found within the facilities covered by this Code are classified as Group IIA.
‡ When the extent of a hazardous area is being classified, consideration shall be given to possible variations in the locating of tank cars and tank vehicles at the unloading points and the effect these variations of locating may have on the point of connection.
§ Where specified for the prevention of fire or explosion during normal operation, ventilation is considered adequate where provided in accordance with the provisions of this Code.
Note 1) For additions to, modifications of, renovations to, or operation and maintenance of existing facilities employing the Division system of classification for Class I locations, the Division system of classification may continue in use.
Note 2) When using the Division system, for the purpose of this table, the following minimum hazardous location classification substitution may be used: a) Class I, Division 1 = for areas classified Zone 1; and b) Class I, Division 2 = for areas classified Zone 2. Zone 1 rated equipment is not permitted in Division 1 locations; for additional information, see Canadian Electrical Code, Part I.

title: Storage of containers at filling plants
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Indoor cylinder storage
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A cylinder may be stored in a container filling and storage building that meets the requirements of Clause 7.17.3.

title: Container fenced area
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When stored outdoors, a container shall be inside the fenced area of a filling plant and shall a) be stored 25 ft (7.6 m) or more from a line of adjoining property; b) be stored not less than 4 ft (1 m) from the fence when the fence has openings greater than 2 x 2 in (50 x 50 mm), but may be stored alongside a fence when the fence has openings 2 x 2 in (50 x 50 mm) or smaller; and c) not be stored within 25 ft (7.6 m) of a filing plant storage tank.

title: Fencing of filling plants
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Fencing requirements
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A filling plant shall be enclosed with a fence constructed in accordance with Clauses 6.5.2.2 and 6.5.2.3. At least two gates through the fencing shall be provided, and the fence shall enclose and be located not less than 10 ft (3 m) from a) a container filling room or container filling location; b) any loading and unloading outlet for a cargo liner or tank truck; c) any pump or compressor; and d) any filing plant storage tank.

title: Location of tanks at filling plants and container refill centres
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Tank location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description:
A tank shall be located in accordance with Table 7.5.

[Table 7.5]
Table 7.5 - Location of tanks at filling plants and container refill centres
(See Clauses 7.16 and 7.19.1.1.)
| Water capacity per tank, USWG (L) | Min. distance, ft (m), between tank and property line; adjacent concrete or masonry building wall with no building openings or windows within the specified clearances; or source of ignition | Min. distance, ft (m), between tank and building openings, windows, or building wall other than concrete or masonry | Min. distance, ft (m), between tank and adjacent tank* | Min. distance, ft (m), between tank and near side of main rail line† |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Up to and including 2000 (7500) | 10 (3) | 25 (7.6) | 3 (0.9) | 25 (7.6) |
| Over 2000 (7500) up to and including 5000 (19 000) | 15 (5)‡ | 25 (7.6) | 3 (0.9) | 100 (30) |
| Over 5000 (19 000) up to and including 10 000 (38 000) | 25 (7.6)‡ | 25 (7.6) | 3 (0.9) | 100 (30) |
| Over 10 000 (38 000) up to and including 30 000 (113 550) | 50 (15.2)‡ | 50 (15.2) | 5 (1.5) | 100 (30) |
| Over 30 000 (113 550) up to and including 70 000 (264 950) | 75 (22.5)‡ | 75 (22.5) | 1/4 of the sum of diameters of adjacent tanks | 100 (30) |
| Over 70 000 (264 950) up to and including 90 000 (340 650) | 100 (30)‡ | 100 (30) | 1/4 of the sum of diameters of adjacent tanks | 100 (30) |
| Over 90 000 (340 650) | 125 (37.5)‡ | 125 (37.5) | 1/4 of the sum of diameters of adjacent tanks | 100 (30) |
* If tanks of a multiple tank installation are installed on a common base or pier, the clearance may be reduced at the discretion of the authority having jurisdiction.
† Where applicable, the requirements of Clause 7.18.2 shall apply.
‡ These distances are subject to the prior approval of the authority having jurisdiction, which may amend the spacing requirement.
Note: A building does not include a container filling or storage building, a pump room, or a building housing an indirect vaporizer.

title: Container filling locations and buildings with provisions for container storage
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A container filling location or building with provisions for container storage shall be located not less than a) 10 ft (3 m) from any tank; b) 25 ft (7.6 m) from a line of adjoining property; and c) 25 ft (7.6 m) from any source of ignition.

title: Pump and compressor location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A pump and compressor may be located in a container filling building, provided that this building is located in accordance with Clause 7.17.1 and constructed in accordance with Clause 7.17.3.

title: Building construction
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A container filling or storage building, a building containing a pump or a compressor shall a) be built of or covered with noncombustible materials, except that rafters, floor, and studs that are exposed to the interior may be of wood; b) be ventilated with openings to the outside air in at least two walls, exclusive of doors, at floor level and at or near the ceiling of the room equally on each of the two walls. Each opening shall have an opening to floor area ratio of not less than 1:600. These ventilation openings shall be not less than 10 ft (3 m) horizontally from any air intake of any appliance or air-moving equipment or any other opening into any building; c) have floor at or above grade. Any space below a floor shall be well ventilated and kept free of combustible material or the space shall be filled in with tamped earth; d) have two or more exits where the travel distance to an exit is more than 15 ft (4.5 m). Additional exits shall be provided when the distance between exits in the same wall is more than 75 ft (22.5 m): i) exit doors shall swing to open outward; and ii) any latch on an exit door shall be only of the magnetic or friction type; and e) have explosion relieving to the outdoors of not less than 1 ft2 (0.1 m2) for each 50 ft3 (1.4 m3) of building volume. The type of explosion relief venting shall be one of the following: i) an open area; ii) an outwardly opening sash; iii) an explosion-relief panel; or iv) single-strength glass.

title: Filling plants served by rail
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Located on private property
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When a distribution location or filling plant is located on private property but served by rail, the requirements of Clause 7.16 shall apply in conjunction with the TC requirements for the siding and unloading tower.

title: Located on railway property
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When a distributing location or filling plant is located on railway property, TC requirements shall apply.

title: Transfer of propane
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When a filling plant is used for the transfer of propane from a rail car to tank truck, tank trailer, or cargo liner, it shall a) satisfy the requirements of Clauses 7.12 to 7.18 and 7.19.4; and b) be approved by the authority having jurisdiction.

title: Container refill centres
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A tank shall be located in accordance with Table 7.5.

title: Storage of containers
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A container refill centre shall meet the requirements of Clauses 6.5.1 and 6.5.2 if it is used for the storage of containers.

title: Aggregate capacity of tanks
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: The aggregate capacity of tanks installed in accordance with Clause 7.19 shall not exceed 5000 USWG (19 000 L).

title: Locked access
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A ventilated cabinet, an enclosure, or fencing used in conjunction with a tank shall be locked when unattended.

title: Service valve
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A service valve normally located under the dome of a tank shall a) have the outlet opening plugged unless it is to be used i) for vapour withdrawal service; or ii) as a connection for the pump bypass piping; and b) not be used for both vapour withdrawal service and as a connection for the pump bypass piping at the same time.

title: Fire extinguisher
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: At least one portable fire extinguisher of not less than 20-B:C rating shall be installed in a readily accessible location at a container refill centre.

title: Emergency shut-off
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A container refill centre shall be equipped with a clearly identified and readily accessible means to shut off the propane system and electrical power to the propane pump and propane dispenser during an emergency.

title: Kiosk doorway
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A kiosk doorway shall not be located within 10 ft (3 m) of any point of transfer it faces.

title: Fencing of container refill centres
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When protection for the primary valves and other transfer equipment of a tank is not provided by a cabinet or enclosure constructed in accordance with Clause 7.19.3, the tank shall be enclosed by fencing, constructed in accordance with Clause 6.5.2.2 or 6.5.2.3, which shall be located not less than 10 ft (3 m) from any point of transfer it faces.

title: Ventilated cabinets or enclosures
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: When a tank is used in conjunction with a ventilated cabinet or enclosure housing dispensing equipment, a) only its relief valves, gauging device, Schedule 80 bypass pipe, and filler valve shall be permitted to be located outside of the cabinet or enclosure; and b) the manually operated internal safety control valve shall provide a means to prevent closure of the cabinet door when the valve is open, except that an alternative means of shut-off may be used that ensures the internal safety control valve remains closed unless the propane dispenser's safety control valve is activated.

title: Post protection
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Posts used for the protection of a tank shall a) be spaced not more than 54 in (1350 mm) apart; b) be buried not less than 36 in (900 mm) below grade; c) extend at least 30 in (750 mm) above grade; and d) be one of the following: i) 4 in (100 mm) capped steel pipe; ii) 4 in (100 mm) capped fully filled with concrete; iii) 8 in (200 mm) pressure-treated wood, either square or round; or iv) 6 in (150 mm) minimum dimension reinforced concrete.

title: Guardrail location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Guardrails used for the protection of a tank shall be a) of the steel deep beam type, 12 x 162 in (300 x 4050 mm), supported by at least two 6 in (150 mm) minimum pressure-treated wooden posts or at least two W6X8.5 (W150X14) minimum galvanized I-beam steel posts buried not less than 36 in (900 mm) below grade and located not more than 75 in (1875 mm) apart, centre-to-centre, and the bottom of the beam shall be 18 in (450 mm) above grade; or b) of the reinforced concrete barrier type, commonly referred to as the New Jersey Turnpike barrier, not less than 30 in (750 mm) in height. See Figure G.1 for an illustration of a typical barrier such as Ontario Provincial Standard Drawing precast concrete barrier (OPSD-920.010 or 911 series) and the maximum spacing between the barriers shall not exceed 54 in (1380 mm).

title: Post or guardrail location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Posts or guardrails used for the protection of a tank shall be located a) not less than 42 in (1080 mm) from all sides of the tank, ventilated cabinet, or enclosure; and b) to ensure that a minimum of 42 in (1080 mm) will be maintained between the point of transfer or cylinder-filling scale and any source of ignition.

title: Other barriers
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Barriers, other than those specified in Clauses 7.19.4.1 and 7.19.4.2, that provide an equivalent level of protection may be used. Equivalency may be demonstrated by physical testing or engineering analysis.

title: Buildings or sites under construction, repair, or renovation
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Protection of tanks used to supply propane to buildings or sites under construction, repair, or improvement may be accomplished by the installation of posts, guardrails, or reinforced concrete barriers as required in Clauses 7.19.4.1, 7.19.4.2, and 7.19.4.3 or by locating a) concrete castings, weighing at least 900 lb (410 kg) and not less than 30 in (750 mm) in height. Any opening between barriers shall not exceed 54 in (1350 mm); b) a continuous berm at a height a minimum of 36 in (900 mm); or c) other means of protection that are equivalent to the protection requirements of Clauses 7.19.4.1, 7.19.4.2, and 7.19.4.3 that are demonstrated by physical testing or engineering analysis. See Annex R for typical illustrations for distances between barriers and tanks.

title: Electrical equipment
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures located at a storage tank, filling building or area, pump, compressor, point of transfer, or similar location shall be classified in accordance with Table 7.6.

title: Dispensing system
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres

title: Dispensing system location
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A propane dispensing system shall be located at least a) 10 ft (3 m) from i) a property line; ii) a drainage basin or ditch; iii) any noncombustible building wall; and b) 25 ft (7.6 m) from a combustible building wall unless approved fire protection is provided.

title: Dispenser located on an island
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A propane dispenser or vehicle fuel dispenser located on an island shall be protected from damage with the use of posts a) as stipulated in Clause 7.19.4.1, items b) to d); and b) located to provide a minimum of i) 1 ft (300 mm) of clear space between the posts and the vehicle fuel dispenser; and ii) 42 in (1080 mm) of clear space between the posts and propane dispenser located on an island that incorporates a cylinder filling scale. As an alternative to items a) and b), a propane dispenser or vehicle fuel dispenser located on an island and protected by posts on a steel base shall be installed where the pump and the internal valve are interlocked so that the valve is closed when the pump is not running, and the ends of the island shall be protected with posts in accordance with Clause 7.19.4.1. The island shall be made of concrete and not less than 4 in (100 mm) high. When propane equipment is provided with a propane dispenser, a vent pipe shall be installed to discharge the vapours to a location at least 10 ft (3 m) from a source of ignition. Note: Water injection of vehicle equipment is not normally considered a source of ignition when the dispensing cone, provided that the engine and any source of ignition is turned off before and during the refuelling. The person who is filling or purging any propane container is responsible for ensuring that there is no source of ignition within 10 ft (3 m) measured horizontally.

title: Below grade piping system located on an island
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: A piping system, located below grade, from a tank to a propane dispenser located on an island shall be installed a) underground in accordance with the provisions of Clause 6.15 of CSA B149.1; or b) in a trough (e.g., as shown in Figure C.1 of this Code). Piping installed in the concrete trough shall be protected from corrosion and comply with the requirements of Clause 6 of CSA B149.1, with the exception of Clause 6.15.

title: Internal safety control valve and shut-off device
section_title: Tank systems, filling plants, and refill centres
description: Except as specified in Clause 7.19.3 b), and except when the dispensing system is activated, the internal safety control valve serving the propane or vehicle fuel dispenser shall remain closed. The propane or vehicle fuel dispenser and internal safety control valve shall be interlocked either electrically or pneumatically. Other means of shutting off the fuel system shall be used on the piping system serving the propane dispenser or vehicle fuel dispenser when closure of the internal safety control valve cannot interrupt other services. This shut-off device shall be located as close as practical to the tank and interlocked with the propane dispenser or vehicle fuel dispenser as above.

title: General
section_title: Tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners
description: All tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners shall be designed, fabricated, and marked in accordance with the requirements of CSA B620.

title: Electrical equipment and lighting on tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners
section_title: Tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners

title: Lighting
section_title: Tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners
description: A tank truck, tank trailer, or cargo liner shall not be equipped with illumination other than that from an electric source. Lighting circuits shall have overcurrent protection, and the wiring shall have sufficient current-carrying capacity to accommodate the electrical load. The wiring shall be secured, grommetted, and protected against damage.

title: Electrical equipment
section_title: Tank trucks, tank trailers, and cargo liners
description: Electric motors, wiring, equipment, and fixtures enclosed in the cabinet shall conform with the provincial or territorial electrical code or the Canadian Electrical Code, Part I, for Class I, Zone 2, Group IIA hazardous locations.

title: General
section_title: Vaporizers

title: Protection
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A vaporizer shall be protected against tampering and damage.

title: Tank heaters
section_title: Vaporizers
description: Vaporizers that are designed to return heated propane to the storage container shall be considered tank heaters.

title: Building drain
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A building used to house a vaporizer shall not have a drain to a sewer or sump pit.

title: Building requirements
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A building or room used for propane vaporizing or mixing shall a) comply with the requirements of Clause 7.17.3, except that the floor of the building shall be built of noncombustible materials; and b) have all pressure relief valve discharges vented to the exterior and shall not terminate within 3 ft (0.9 m) of a ventilation opening unless the relief discharges vertically upward and is above the roof line.

title: Coil location
section_title: Vaporizers
description: Heating or cooling coils shall not be installed inside a container.

title: Valves
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A vaporizer shall have a manually operated valve in each connection to a container.

title: Maximum pressure
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A vaporizer shall not raise the pressure in a container in excess of 215 psig (1.48 MPa).

title: Liquid carryover
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A vaporizer shall be provided with suitable automatic means to prevent propane in the liquid phase from passing from the vaporizer to the vapour discharge piping.

title: Source of ignition
section_title: Vaporizers
description: An open flame or other source of ignition shall not be permitted in a building housing a vaporizer unless the vaporizer is a direct-fired vaporizer.

title: Emergency shutdown
section_title: Vaporizers
description: An emergency shutdown system shall be installed in any tank system supplying propane to a direct-fired vaporizer. The emergency shutdown system may be of the electrical, pneumatic, or mechanical type or a combination thereof and the means to activate the emergency shutdown system shall be located at least 25 ft (7.6 m) from the direct-fired vaporizer. The emergency shutdown system when activated shall initiate shut-off of the liquid line at the tank location that is connected to the vaporizer.

title: Indirect vaporizers
section_title: Vaporizers

title: Pressure vessel registration
section_title: Vaporizers
description: An indirect vaporizer classified as a pressure vessel shall be designed, constructed, tested, and marked in accordance with CSA B51 and the regulations of the boiler and pressure vessel inspection authority of the province or territory in which it is installed.

title: Design working pressure
section_title: Vaporizers
description: An indirect vaporizer with an inside diameter of 6 in (150 mm) or less that is exempted from the requirements of CSA B51 shall have a design working pressure of not less than 250 psig (1.72 MPa) and need not be permanently marked.

title: Direct-fired vaporizers
section_title: Vaporizers

title: Location restrictions
section_title: Vaporizers
description: Subject to Clause 9.3.4, a direct-fired vaporizer shall not be permitted in a pumphouse, a container filling or storage room, or in any other building or room.

title: Source of ignition
section_title: Vaporizers
description: A direct-fired vaporizer is considered a source of ignition and shall be located at a distance in accordance with the requirements of Table 7.4 from any tank or from the fill points of any tank, and at least 25 ft (7.6 m) from a building or property line.
